headline: Prime Video's 'Summer I Turned Pretty' Crew Pleads for Set Secrecy
lede: Amazon's streaming hit is drawing passionate fan attention that's threatening production logistics. The series' cast and creative team are now publicly requesting boundaries.
body:
Prime Video's wildly popular teen drama 'The Summer I Turned Pretty' is facing an unexpected challenge: overzealous fan engagement that's disrupting on-location filming.

The streamer released a strategic social media appeal this week, asking fans to respect production boundaries and maintain the series' creative mystique. While fan enthusiasm typically signals a show's cultural resonance, the current level of location tracking and set visits threatens to derail shooting schedules.

Production sources suggest fans have been aggressively pursuing filming locations, potentially compromising actor safety and narrative integrity. The series, based on Jenny Han's beloved young adult novels, has cultivated an exceptionally passionate fanbase since its 2022 debut.

Streamers increasingly confront this digital-era challenge: balancing fan engagement with production protection. Social media's real-time location sharing can transform fan excitement into logistical nightmares for production teams seeking authenticity and actor comfort.

Prime Video's request signals a broader industry trend of establishing clearer fan interaction protocols. Showrunners and network communications teams are developing more nuanced strategies to channel fan energy without compromising creative processes.
key_facts: Fans tracking 'Summer I Turned Pretty' filming locations
Prime Video requests privacy for production
Series based on Jenny Han's novels
